name: Safe-Life vs Damage-Tolerance
summary: Two structural-certification philosophies: either retire the part before cracks are expected, or prove the structure can survive detectable damage until inspection catches it.
whenToUseIt: Use it when choosing inspection strategy, structural margins, and certification evidence for fatigue-critical aerospace structures.
steps: Classify the structure and identify critical failure modes.
Estimate fatigue loading, crack initiation risk, and consequence of failure.
Decide whether the component should be life-limited or inspectable in service.
Set inspection intervals, allowable damage sizes, and retirement criteria.
Verify the approach with test evidence and regulatory agreement.
example: A landing-gear fitting may be managed with a safe-life retirement interval, while a fuselage skin panel may use damage-tolerance analysis because cracks can be detected and repaired during scheduled maintenance.
